name: Q2581463
abstract: William Ralph Maiden (March 12, 1928 – May 29, 1976) was an American jazz saxophonist and arranger. Maiden began on piano at age five and started playing saxophone at 11.

concerts.label: Jack Humphrey Septet/ Maynard Ferguson and His Orchestra/ Sarah Vaughan/ Modern Jazz Quartet
Midnight Performance: Jack Humphrey Septet/ Maynard Ferguson and His Orchestra/ Sarah Vaughan/ Modern Jazz Quartet

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Erskine
about_quotes.evidence: Stan commanded much love and respect from his team of writers, Willie Maiden being among them... Willie was a funny guy... Willie was on the band
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Erskine interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Orleans, Louisiana, January 9, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Lanny Morgan
about_quotes.evidence: Willie Maiden was an old friend of mine. And he was playing baritone on the band... I was staying with Willie Maiden.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lanny Morgan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Carmen Leggio
about_quotes.evidence: I was in the original band... Jimmy Ford, Willie Maiden, and Bill Chase was on it
about_quotes.doc_title: Carmen Leggio interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: Willie Maiden and Don Sebesky and a couple of other guys that were in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton interviewed by Monk Rowe, West Orange, New Jersey, July 30, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: it did give me a chance to work with Lanny Morgan and Willie Maiden
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Vax
about_quotes.evidence: Willie Maiden, rest his soul, said to Stan... 'These young kids are playing your music as good as anybody ever has' ... Willie Maiden brings some of the bigger guys back in there
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Vax interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 29, 2022
